title: christensonella neowiedii
side_effects: Christensonella neowiedii can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its toxic compounds. Ingestion of large amounts may lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach pain. Improper use of the plant can also cause allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to its toxic components can result in organ damage and even death in extreme cases.
